title: Syracuse councilors Hogan & Majok oppose proposed tax hike amid reserve surplus
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh proposed a $348.4M 2026 budget with 2% property tax and water bill increases, but Councilors Pat Hogan and Chol Majok oppose the hike citing the city's $120M reserve fund surplus—which has doubled since Walsh took office. City auditors note the unassigned fund balance is unprecedented at ~45-50% of annual expenditure versus the typical 15-20% benchmark, raising questions about fiscal necessity.

political_signal.description: Council members directly challenge Mayor Walsh's tax increase proposal despite substantial municipal reserves, signaling fiscal scrutiny and potential constituent pressure against property tax burden.

processing_notes: Article date 2025-04-10 is future-dated; verify against publication metadata.
Dollar amount $120M refers to reserve fund (doubled), $348.4M is total budget; $2M and $10K are sponsored content artifacts.
Three councilors named (Hogan, Majok, Nave) as opponents; Nave mentioned but not directly quoted.
External auditor Greg Evans cited; source of audit report not linked in article.
Water utility increase bundled with property tax increase—may need separate tracking.
